focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus reveals a hidden door, claims Martin is gone, and mocks the policeman; Percy defuses tension while the officer threatens the group and subdues Martin's friend.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reasserts authority after emerging from a trance, reveals a hidden door behind the mirror, and confronts a police officer with defiance and mockery.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain control of the Imaginarium and protect its secrets
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Doctor Parnassus regains physical and situational control, reestablishing leadership over the troupe and asserting dominance in the confrontation with authorities
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Martin's fate and the nature of the mirror world remain unclear
evidence_quotes: DR. PARNASSUS rises unsteadily, coming out of his arduous trance.
`Beauty` moves to support him as, exhausted, he loses his balance and snaps out of his trance.
DR. PARNASSUS pushes aside the mirror to reveal a door in the back wall of the shallow stage.

relation_updates: strained father-daughter dynamic with Valentina revealed
adversarial stance established with the policeman
status_updates: back in active control of the situation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus's troupe performs at a chaotic fair; a boy named Diego slips through the mirror into the Imaginarium, prompting Anton to follow while Valentina distracts the crowd.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Interrupts his trance to confront a disruptive audience, but fails to notice a child entering the mirror during the distraction.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain focus and respect for the Imaginarium performance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: loses control over the performance environment, allowing a child to enter the Imaginarium unattended
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: a child has entered the Imaginarium unattended
evidence_quotes: DR. PARNASSUS stirs from his trance. He's had enough of this.
DR. PARNASSUS Please! This is The Imaginarium! It requires respect, concentration... Kindly go elsewhere and take your detritus with you?
Unnoticed by all, DIEGO has somehow managed to climb onto the stage and is peering at the mirror.
She mimes that a certain someone has gone into the mirror.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 15、EXT EDGE OF FAIR / THE WAGON CONTINUOUS
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mr. Nick appears, laughing, while Dr. Parnassus grows distressed, fearing Nick has come to collect a debt; Percy grimly acknowledges it and urges Parnassus to tell Valentina.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mr. Nick appears unexpectedly, causing Doctor Parnassus to realize the supernatural debt from his past is now due, leading to immediate distress and fear over Valentina's safety.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: avoid Nick's collection and protect Valentina
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally distressed and overwhelmed, facing the imminent consequence of his past bargain
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to prevent Mr. Nick from claiming what is owed
evidence_quotes: The horse lifts its head and DR PARNASSUS recoils in shock and horror. Standing directly behind the horse is MR. NICK...
MR NICK Hello Parnassus... It's been a while hasn't it?
DOCTOR PARNASSUS (distraught) He's come to collect.

relation_updates: fearful awareness of Mr. Nick's claim

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A bird's droppings on Mr. Nick reignite Dr. Parnassus's belief that stories sustain the universe, leading to a bet with the devil after proving storytelling persists worldwide.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Regains conviction that storytelling sustains the universe after a bird defecates on Mr. Nick, interpreting it as a sign that stories continue elsewhere.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to prove that stories sustain the universe
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: enters into a wager with Mr. Nick after demonstrating that storytelling persists globally
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the specific terms of the wager are not revealed
evidence_quotes: DR. PARNASSUS A sign! A message! That bird was a messenger... from distant places we know not of!
Other places! The point is, you're wrong! And I'm wrong! It doesn't have to be us here!
Somewhere in the world, at any given time... someone is telling a story! Sustaining the universe! Right now, it's happening.
DR. PARNASSUS (V.O.) And so I made a wager with the devil.

relation_updates: adversarial tension with Mr. Nick escalates
status_updates: renewed conviction in storytelling's cosmic role
auxiliary.persona_anchor: defiant believer in the power of stories

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy tries to stop Dr. Parnassus from jumping off a cliff; Parnassus falls but is saved by Mr. Nick, who holds him by his coat snagged on a branch.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Attempts to jump from a cliff in despair over his age and failed love, but is physically saved by Mr. Nick after his coat snags on a branch.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: rescued but left dangling and helpless, publicly humiliated and dependent on Mr. Nick
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he can continue his mission after this failure
evidence_quotes: PERCY is struggling with DR PARNASSUS, trying to prevent him from throwing himself off the cliff.
DR. PARNASSUS breaks free, stumbles backwards, and pitches out into space.
DR. PARNASSUS V.O. At times like these... the devil is never far away.

relation_updates: Mr. Nick intervenes at a moment of crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A man with mysterious forehead marks is mistaken by Doctor Parnassus as an emissary from Mr. Nick bearing a proposal; Valentina approves, Percy doubts, and the man plays along to stay.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Identifies the rescued man as a sign from Mr. Nick after matching his belongings to The Hanged Man tarot card.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: begins to understand the man is a sign from Mr. Nick
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: accepts the supernatural challenge is active
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what Mr. Nick's new proposal entails
evidence_quotes: DR. PARNASSUS, holding a cold, damp towel to his throbbing head, appears, coughing and miserable from the wagon.
DOCTOR PARNASSUS The hanged man!

relation_updates: shifts from dismissal to intense interest in the man
status_updates: moves from physical discomfort to focused realization

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Mr. Nick taunts Doctor Parnassus with a bet to save Valentina by her birthday, claiming business will improve, then leaves abruptly.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Mr. Nick proposes a bet requiring Doctor Parnassus to acquire five souls by Valentina's birthday in exchange for her life, reigniting their spiritual contest.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: acquire five souls before Valentina's birthday to win the bet against Mr. Nick
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Parnassus is desperate but now has a clear mission
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he can succeed against overwhelming odds
evidence_quotes: DOCTOR PARNASSUS What do you want? I'll do anything.
MR NICK I just thought...seeing you so upset last night...that you needed a glimmer of hope...maybe a little bet?
First to five?... You might win. And save her. What do you have to lose?
By her birthday then.

relation_updates: adversarial tension with Mr. Nick escalates
status_updates: formally enters a time-bound spiritual contest

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony leads women through the mirror, donating valuables, while Parnassus nears freedom. Nick observes, unimpressed. Russians recognize Tony, chase him through the mirror, disrupting the show and chasing him into the fantastical realm.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Celebrates four successful redemptions and signals triumph to Mr. Nick, anticipating final redemption.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: achieve five redemptions to secure freedom and win the bet
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: stands on the verge of victory as external chaos begins to disrupt the performance
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the arrival of the Russians threatens to derail the final redemption and destabilize the show
evidence_quotes: DOCTOR PARNASSUS (ignoring him and with a big theatrical gesture) Hooplah!
PARNASSUS steps forward and helps her to her feet. She kisses his hands in gratitude.
DR. PARNASSUS (aside to Percy) One more to go and we're free.
DOCTOR PARNASSUS spots MR NICK across the street, smiles, holds up four fingers.

relation_updates: challenges Mr. Nick with a gesture of triumph
status_updates: on the verge of fulfilling his destiny
auxiliary.persona_anchor: ritualistic, confident, close to fulfilling his purpose

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus proposes a new wager to free Valentina, but she rejects him, angry over his lies and arrogance, and flees, leaving him desperate as Tony watches from the shadows.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reveals to Valentina that she belongs to the Devil as part of a past wager and proposes a new wager to save her, but she rejects him in anger and disbelief.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to convince Valentina of the truth and initiate a new wager to save her from the Devil
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally broken and abandoned by Valentina after she flees in anger
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Valentina refuses to believe his revelation and sees him as arrogant and manipulative
evidence_quotes: I was mortal again... on condition that... any children I fathered... would, on their sixteenth birthday... belong to him.
VALENTINA I don't believe this! It can't be true. It's just another of your wretched stories... A cruel story!
A new wager... The first of five. If I win... you're free... you're mine again.. and we can win!

relation_updates: relationship with Valentina ruptures as she rejects him

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina chooses the 'His' mirror, dances with Mr. Nick in a surreal tango, and passes through it despite his attempts to stop her, leaving him realizing he's been tricked as Parnassus calls out.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Confronts Tony in the mirror world, declares himself Valentina's father, and is trampled by the mob after redirecting them toward Tony, resulting in his apparent death.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect Valentina from Tony
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: lies motionless and presumed dead after being trampled, while Valentina is overwhelmed with grief
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his actual survival and potential reunion with Valentina
evidence_quotes: DR. PARNASSUS(O.S.) Valentina! My darling daughter! There you are!
Throwing his arm around TONY, PARNASSUS spins him around to face the MOB rushing towards him.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus, believed dead by Valentina, returns; she lets him in but shuts Percy out, focusing solely on her father.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reunites with Valentina after being presumed dead, but her focus remains on her new life as a mother, creating emotional distance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reconnect with his daughter
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: acknowledged as father, but outsider in her new life
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to rebuild relationship after years of absence
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA Father...? (recovering) Where have you been?... All these years... I thought you were dead.
Both DR. PARNASSUS and VALENTINA are astonished. Neither knows what to say.

relation_updates: Valentina lets him in but rejects Percy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy and Dr. Parnassus run a toy theatre stall; during a performance, a boy asks about a happy ending, which Dr. Parnassus cannot guarantee, as Mr. Nick watches from the shadows.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Sells a toy theatre and, when asked, declines to promise a happy ending, reflecting his acceptance of uncertainty in stories and life.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: reinforces his stance that outcomes cannot be guaranteed, even as Mr. Nick observes silently
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Mr. Nick's presence implies the ongoing influence of their wager and the unresolved spiritual stakes
evidence_quotes: He and DR. PARNASSUS are running a stall selling toy theatres.
DR. PARNASSUS takes the money and expertly wraps the theatre.
DR. PARNASSUS Beautifully made. It'll last for years if properly looked after.
NINE YEAR OLD SON Does it come with a happy ending?

relation_updates: Mr. Nick reappears as a looming presence
Percy remains a cooperative partner in performance

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Doctor Parnassus, an immortal mystic bound by a fateful wager with the devil, struggles to protect his daughter Valentina from a predestined fate while maintaining the relevance of his Imaginarium. His journey traces a decline from authoritative showman to broken father, marked by failed attempts to control supernatural forces, confront his past, and reclaim his daughter. After a series of losses and revelations, he ultimately accepts a diminished role in a changed world, continuing his storytelling without guarantees. The arc reflects a sustained tension between mythic duty and human vulnerability.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony demands his pipe back from Anton, who teases him by making it disappear; Valentina intervenes, giving the pipe to Tony, while Dr. Parnassus observes thoughtfully.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony learns his real name is Anthony Shepherd and that he once did charity work.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking self-knowledge
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony now knows his name and some of his past, but has no memory of how he ended up under the bridge.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tony has no memory of how he ended up under the bridge.
evidence_quotes: Your name.
Anthony Shepherd.
Tony Shepherd! That's who I am!
And... you used to do good works. Something to do with charity I think.

relation_updates: Valentina views Tony as a good person
status_updates: Tony transitions from amnesiac to self-aware

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony demands his pipe back from Anton, who teases him by making it disappear; Valentina intervenes, giving the pipe to Tony, while Dr. Parnassus observes thoughtfully.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony insists the metal pipe belongs to him and demands it back from Anton, showing clear emotional concern over its return.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: recovering a meaningful possession
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony obtains the pipe after Valentina takes it from Anton and gives it to him.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason why the pipe is important to Tony remains unexplained.
evidence_quotes: It's mine.
May I have it?... Please.
Don't fool around.
Give it to me!

relation_updates: Valentina sides with Tony against Anton
status_updates: Tony gains possession of the pipe

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Percy fires shots, secures the theater, and escapes with Tony on a wagon; Sally is thrown out and found by Mr. Nick, who helps her up as the wagon flees.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony is attacked by the mob after trying to calm them, knocked off the stage, and beaten until Percy's gunfire creates an opening for escape.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to protect the group and prevent chaos
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: injured and fleeing on a moving wagon
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the mob is pursuing them and may alert the police
evidence_quotes: TONY and PERCY try to hold them back.
TONY pleads with the crowd, insisting everything is alright, urging calm, and promising that there is no cause for alarm.
A MIDDLE AGED WOMAN strikes him with her handbag, escalating the tension.
A DRUNK takes a swing at PERCY but misses and hits TONY instead,

relation_updates: gains reliance on Valentina for rescue

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anton is moved by Valentina's plea; she smiles and asks him how much money he has, shifting focus to their escape resources.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony proposes changes to modernize the troupe's show and strengthens his emotional bond with Valentina through physical care and intimacy.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reform the Imaginarium to attract a new audience and prove his value to the group
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony is accepted as a decision-maker within the troupe and becomes emotionally close to Valentina, though his authority is initially challenged.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his past remains unclear, and his sudden influence causes tension among the group members
evidence_quotes: In the shadow of the wagon VALENTINA is changing TONY'S dressings.
TONY If you hadn't been there... He holds her hand. Looks deep into her eyes.
TONY puts his arms around her. Holds her tightly to him.
PERCY Change the show!? Who the fizzing hell do you think you are?......

relation_updates: grows closer to Valentina through physical intimacy and emotional support
asserts authority to Doctor Parnassus and Percy, initiating a power shift
status_updates: emerges as a strategic leader within the troupe

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony selects a woman for the Imaginarium, offering entry based on dreams, not payment; as she and Tony enter, police arrive but delay action upon seeing Valentina, allowing the show to continue.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony publicly redefines the Imaginarium as a non-transactional space, returning money and describing it as a refuge for souls in need of truth.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: attract the right audience through emotional appeal rather than transaction
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The audience becomes pensive and engaged, shifting from transactional expectation to emotional consideration, allowing the performance to continue amid police presence.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his past remains hidden, and the arriving police pose a threat of exposure
evidence_quotes: TONY (retrieving the money) Excuse me, madam. I'm afraid we can't accept this.
We are here to give...not take. We are here to help those in need.
Cost? Money is no good in the Imaginarium. This is a refuge for hearts in need of truth. A place for souls to be purified.
Madam, why should you care about time? What are your dreams worth in minutes?

relation_updates: builds rapport with a wealthy woman through empathetic engagement
demonstrates leadership that astonishes the troupe
status_updates: becomes the public face of the Imaginarium
commands attention in a high-stakes urban setting

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony leads women through the mirror, donating valuables, while Parnassus nears freedom. Nick observes, unimpressed. Russians recognize Tony, chase him through the mirror, disrupting the show and chasing him into the fantastical realm.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony becomes increasingly flamboyant during the recruitment performance, fails to wear his mask properly, and is recognized by Russians who begin chasing him, prompting his escape through the mirror.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: recruiting women for transformation to aid Parnassus
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: flees into the mirror world to escape pursuit
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his past identity is exposed, drawing immediate danger
evidence_quotes: TONY, getting more and more flamboyant, forgets to lower his mask each time he re-enters the stage.
SERGE (IN RUSSIAN) I'm sure.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Another knock heightens tension; Percy anxiously urges answering, unable to bear the suspense, while Parnassus steps out to confront the visitor, leaving the others uneasy.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony admits he accepted a loan from people he did not know were part of a crime syndicate, which was used to fund his charity, and expresses regret for the mistake.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to prove his essential goodness and clear his name
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Valentina shows sympathy, but Anton and Percy remain distrustful
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Tony is innocent or complicit in the crime syndicate's activities
evidence_quotes: I am not a liar!
I just... didn't divulge everything I knew.
I - with hindsight very foolishly - accepted a loan from people who seemed to me, at the time, to be very decent and sincere.

relation_updates: deepens distrust with Anton and Percy

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony, accused of past crimes, drags Valentina into a mirror to escape exposure, shoving Anton offstage; Parnassus, in a trance, pleads for forgiveness as the truth unravels.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony takes decisive action to save Valentina by pushing Parnassus to enter a trance and volunteering to enter the mirror world as the final soul.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Save Valentina and earn her hand in marriage; gain access to Parnassus’s secrets.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony transitions from a passive observer to an active participant, committing to enter the mirror world to rescue Valentina while physically guiding Parnassus into position and urging him to concentrate.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Parnassus can maintain the trance state necessary for the plan to succeed.
evidence_quotes: TONY Saving Valentina.
I'll be that one. I'll take the risk to save Valentina and, hopefully, you sir.
Tony steers DOCTOR PARNASSUS towards his glass column.
Concentrate. You must concentrate.

status_updates: actively driving the mission to save Valentina

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Anton, as a child, exposes Tony's crimes with a newspaper, causing Tony to violently attack him while Valentina tries to intervene, revealing Tony's abuse and hypocrisy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: abusive leader exposed for past crimes
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony's criminal history is revealed by a child version of Anton, leading to a violent outburst in front of others.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain control and prevent exposure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony is publicly challenged and physically violent, beginning the collapse of his authority and image
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to regain control after public exposure and violent reaction
evidence_quotes: ANTON (reading and dodging) 'The disgraced director of the children's charity \"Suffer the Little Children, who was arrested last..
TONY lunges for him but, ANTON dodges out of the way.
TONY has grabbed little ANTON and starts hitting him.
You've betrayed me! Haven't you? You let him in...into my world, you bitch!?

relation_updates: Valentina shifts from ally to opponent after witnessing abuse
Anton becomes an active threat to Tony's authority
status_updates: physical violence escalates in response to challenge

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina chooses the 'His' mirror, dances with Mr. Nick in a surreal tango, and passes through it despite his attempts to stop her, leaving him realizing he's been tricked as Parnassus calls out.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony violently attacks Valentina and frames Parnassus as the ballroom collapses around him, revealing his desperation and disintegration of authority.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: discover who manipulated events against him
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony becomes a fugitive, physically and socially isolated after being chased by the mob and causing Parnassus's death
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he will evade capture or face retribution
evidence_quotes: Behind the choir the double doors of the ballroom crash open... exposing TONY violently beating up an `ethnic child.'
At the same time the walls of the ballroom begin to crack and disintegrate.
The floor around TONY falls away into an abyss leaving only a narrow escape route.
Suddenly, VALENTINA is jerked backwards by her hair.

relation_updates: Valentina is physically assaulted and fully alienated
Parnassus is framed and sacrificed to the mob
status_updates: transition from powerful figure to fugitive

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Parnassus recalls one rule against black magic, forgets others, and confronts Nick about his daughter's whereabouts; Nick mocks him and blows smoke, obscuring the scene.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Tony confronts Parnassus and exposes the devil's lie
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony realizes that killing him would give Mr. Nick his fifth win, securing Valentina's soul for the devil rather than freeing her, and uses this knowledge to challenge Parnassus.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: survive and prevent being used in Parnassus's deal
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony temporarily halts Parnassus's attempt to kill him by revealing the truth about Nick's deception
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Tony's revelation will stop Parnassus or provoke him to act anyway
evidence_quotes: It's all lies. He can't release her. And why not? Because he doesn't have her. He can't have her because she's the prize.
(gaining confidence) He doesn't have five wins. But if you kill me he will, won't he? You'll have lost Valentina forever!

status_updates: Tony recognized as key to Nick's fifth win

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Parnassus recalls one rule against black magic, forgets others, and confronts Nick about his daughter's whereabouts; Nick mocks him and blows smoke, obscuring the scene.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Tony is deceived by Parnassus's fake hanging and captured by the mob
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Tony believes he has recovered the real brass tube after freeing Parnassus, but is overpowered by the mob and hanged when Parnassus reveals he had kept the real tube all along.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: retain the brass tube and survive
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Tony is dead, having been hanged by the mob after losing the tube to Parnassus
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Tony's death impacts the ongoing conflict between Parnassus and Nick
evidence_quotes: He slides the tube down his throat and kicks the chair away. He drops... dangles from the rope...the noose tight around his neck.
Working very fast, TONY hoists him over his shoulder and wrestles the noose loose.
The tube pops out of the DOCTOR'S gullet. TONY catches it.
With a look of realization and horror, TONY drops to his death as the MOB kicks the chair away from under him.

status_updates: Tony loses possession of the brass tube

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Tony begins as an amnesiac under the bridge, gradually recovering his identity as Anthony Shepherd, a former charity figure with a hidden past. He rises within the Imaginarium troupe, becoming a charismatic leader and romantic partner to Valentina, but his facade unravels as his criminal ties and violent tendencies emerge. After being exposed and fleeing into the mirror world, he becomes a pivotal figure in the supernatural contest for Valentina’s soul, ultimately dying in a deception that seals Mr. Nick’s victory.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Drunk Martin harasses Valentina, chases her into the mirror, and disrupts the performance while Anton and Percy hesitate to wake the still-trancing Doctor Parnassus.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina intervenes to stop Martin from disrupting the performance and escapes through the mirror to avoid his advance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: defend the stage and avoid assault
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: enters the Imaginarium to escape Martin's pursuit
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Martin remains unchecked and follows her into the mirror
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA Stop! Get off the stage!
VALENTINA Get off the stage!
MARTIN No. I wan you... I wan you...
MARTIN lunges at `Beauty'

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina punches Martin, leaves him; he chases her, trips on a real tree root, and finds himself in a dark, real forest—Beauty is gone.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina punches Martin in the gut and walks away, choosing not to inflict further harm.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: escape and deter pursuit
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: leaves Martin on the ground, creating distance between them
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Martin remains hostile and attempts to chase her after she leaves
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA stops, turns, smiles sweetly and slams her fist hard into MARTIN'S gut.
He doubles up.
VALENTINA pauses for a moment, contemplating doing some serious damage, before striding off.
MARTIN (CONT'D) Come back! You bitch!

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus reveals a hidden door, claims Martin is gone, and mocks the policeman; Percy defuses tension while the officer threatens the group and subdues Martin's friend.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina argues with Dr. Parnassus about her use of the mirror, asserting it was self-defense after being chased, and challenges him when he blames her behavior on her mother's death.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: defending autonomy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: open conflict with Dr. Parnassus, no reconciliation achieved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: grief over mother's death, rules around mirror use
evidence_quotes: You don't go through the mirror.
I had to get away.
Self defence.
You're out of control. Ever since your mother died...

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus's troupe performs at a chaotic fair; a boy named Diego slips through the mirror into the Imaginarium, prompting Anton to follow while Valentina distracts the crowd.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina takes over the performance when Diego enters the mirror and Anton follows him, maintaining the show under pressure.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: preserving the performance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: onstage as primary performer
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Diego's safety in the Imaginarium
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA turns her head just in time to see DIEGO vanish through the mirror. She starts to go after him, then hesitates.
VALENTINA takes over the pitch. VALENTINA (CONT'D) Ladies and Gentlemen, let Dr. Parnassus be your guide...

status_updates: stepped into lead role during crisis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina reveals she is nearly sixteen, not twelve, and expresses a desire to be ordinary, contrasting her feelings about Parnassus and her teasing relationship with Anton, leaving him thoughtful.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina reveals her true age is nearly sixteen and expresses a desire to live an ordinary life, distancing herself from her father Parnassus's magical world.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to live a normal life
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Valentina asserts her identity as someone who wants to be ordinary, separate from her father's influence and expectations.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: tension between her upbringing in a magical environment and her wish for normalcy
evidence_quotes: Actually, I'm going to be sixteen.
Shall I tell Parnassus I know? I think he knows I know. Silly man. Sweet sixteen.
The age of consent.

relation_updates: teases Anton while maintaining emotional distance
status_updates: age revealed as sixteen, not twelve

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 26、EXT WAGON ON BLACK FRIARS' BRIDGE NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: During a lightning flash, Valentina and Anton see a shadow of a hanged man dancing on the water beneath Blackfriar's Bridge; Valentina panics, demanding a rope to help.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina reacts with horror to the sight of a hanged man and demands a rope to attempt a rescue.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: rescue the hanging man
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: commits to intervention despite uncertainty
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: feasibility of rescuing someone from under a bridge in a storm
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA (horrified) There's someone hanging there!
VALENTINA A rope! Get a rope!

status_updates: shifts from skepticism to active intervention

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 29、EXT BLACK FRIARS' BRIDGE REAR OF WAGON MOMENTS LATER
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina moves the man into the trunk despite Anton's and Percy's objections, showing unexpected care by placing a cushion under his head.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina insists on moving the unconscious man into the trunk despite objections from Anton and Percy, and personally places a cushion under his head.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect and care for the unconscious man
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the man is stowed in the trunk under Valentina's care, with her taking full responsibility for his concealment and safety
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of punishment for disobeying orders and hiding the man
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA tells them to put him in the big trunk.
VALENTINA insists, 'Don't argue.'
VALENTINA argues that the boss thought the man was dead.

status_updates: assumes full responsibility for the man’s safety

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A man with mysterious forehead marks is mistaken by Doctor Parnassus as an emissary from Mr. Nick bearing a proposal; Valentina approves, Percy doubts, and the man plays along to stay.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina expresses concern for the man's well-being and challenges the group's hesitation, advocating for his acceptance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect the vulnerable and maintain group openness
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: the man is allowed to stay, with Valentina's appeal contributing to Doctor Parnassus's decision
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the man's true identity and origins remain unclear
evidence_quotes: Are you alright?
He tried to kill himself.
Why not?
Father?

relation_updates: strengthens trust with Parnassus through appeal

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Parnassus proposes a new wager to free Valentina, but she rejects him, angry over his lies and arrogance, and flees, leaving him desperate as Tony watches from the shadows.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina learns her father made a deal with the Devil, binding her to him on her 16th birthday, and reacts with anger and disbelief before rejecting Dr. Parnassus and fleeing.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reject paternal control and moral hypocrisy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: autonomous, disillusioned, in flight
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to escape her fate or reclaim agency
evidence_quotes: I was mortal again... on condition that... any children I fathered... would, on their sixteenth birthday... belong to him.
VALENTINA I don't believe this! It can't be true. It's just another of your wretched stories... A cruel story!
You're arrogant! Vain and arrogant! I'm just another one of your stupid wagers!... You're beneath contempt.
She rushes off into the dark night.

status_updates: learns she belongs to the Devil on her 16th birthday

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Tony, accused of past crimes, drags Valentina into a mirror to escape exposure, shoving Anton offstage; Parnassus, in a trance, pleads for forgiveness as the truth unravels.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina is confronted with accusations against Tony but is pulled into the mirror by him after he dismisses the claims and removes Anton from the scene.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to believe Tony and escape exposure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: physically taken into the mirror world by Tony, separated from Anton and the real world
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Tony is truthful or evading justice
evidence_quotes: ANTON Ah, there you are, Tony. Here's something m-might interest you. (reading) 'Missing: D-disgraced head of children's charity'.
TONY (CONT'D) (grabbing VALENTINA) Hurry! TONY and VALENTINA disappear through the mirror.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina chooses the 'His' mirror, dances with Mr. Nick in a surreal tango, and passes through it despite his attempts to stop her, leaving him realizing he's been tricked as Parnassus calls out.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina chooses to enter the 'His' mirror despite Mr. Nick's attempts to redirect her, using a mirrored tango to outmaneuver him.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to make her own choice under pressure
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: enters the 'His' mirror realm, evading Mr. Nick's influence
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the nature and consequences of the 'His' path are unclear
evidence_quotes: VALENTINA heads for the mirror marked 'His'.
MR NICK blocks her path.
a kind of dance between them begins - confusingly reflected in the mirrors. A tango can be heard.
She sails through - and, with a mad smile, throws him a kiss.

status_updates: assumes active role in her fate

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Valentina expresses happiness with Anton, presenting their baby Imogen to Dr. Parnassus, who is emotionally overwhelmed, sitting silently with tears as the child's father.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Valentina affirms her contentment with her life as a mother and partner, directly addressing her father's disapproval.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to assert her life choices and find validation in her own terms
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Valentina remains composed and self-assured after sharing her happiness, despite her father's silent emotional reaction.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dr. Parnassus's silence prevents verbal acknowledgment or reconciliation, leaving emotional reciprocity unfulfilled.
evidence_quotes: This is all I ever wanted... I know it's not what you wanted for me, but...well... there you go... I'm happy.
We're both happy.
She's eight months, and, so far, fortunately, doesn't look in the least like you.
He sits there, a ragged old man with a baby on his knee, tears streaming down his face.

relation_updates: emotional distance from Dr. Parnassus is confirmed
solidarity with Anton is publicly affirmed

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Valentina evolves from a defiant performer protecting her father's troupe to a self-aware young woman confronting the supernatural bargains that govern her fate. Her journey traces a path from resistance to disillusionment, culminating in a hard-won assertion of agency. She transitions from being a pawn in her father's and Mr. Nick's game to making an autonomous choice that redefines her identity, ultimately finding peace in a life of her own making.
